summary: PodDisruptionBudget support for the Keystone operator, addressing all 4 items from the sourcery-ai external review: explicit replicas=0 handling in buildPodDisruptionBudget, DeepCopy-then-compare guard to skip no-op updates in EnsurePDB, owner reference enforcement on the update path, and explicit replica count in tests. Implementation spans EnsurePDB helper (internal/common/deployment), buildPodDisruptionBudget + call site (operators/keystone), RBAC marker, Owns() watch, and 14 new tests across both packages.

tests_checklist.item: All existing tests pass (go test ./internal/common/deployment/... — 30 tests)
All existing tests pass (go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/... — PDB tests)
EnsurePDB unit tests: create, update, idempotent, ownerRef reconciliation, label merge, annotation merge
PDB integration tests: creation, labels/selector, minAvailable for replicas>1, maxUnavailable for replicas==1, update on replica change, selector matches Deployment
Tests explicitly set ks.Spec.Replicas where PDB expectations depend on it (CC-0037)

code_quality_checklist.item: EnsurePDB follows get-or-create/update contract with error-only return
Error messages include namespace/name context matching EnsureDeployment/EnsureService
buildPodDisruptionBudget is a pure function matching buildKeystoneDeployment/buildKeystoneService pattern
PDB named {keystone.Name}-api consistent with Deployment and Service naming
SPDX headers present on all files
Feature ID CC-0037 referenced in comments and docstrings

security_checklist.item: RBAC marker scoped to policy group, poddisruptionbudgets resource only
No hardcoded secrets or sensitive data
Owner references ensure garbage collection on CR deletion

architecture_checklist.item: EnsurePDB is a reusable helper in internal/common/deployment (not operator-specific)
Call site is after EnsureService and before DeploymentReady condition check
SetupWithManager includes Owns(&policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{}) for watch reconciliation
PDB uses commonLabels() for ObjectMeta and selectorLabels() for Spec.Selector, matching Deployment

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — EnsurePDB is the single PDB management helper
No unnecessary abstractions — buildPodDisruptionBudget is a focused builder

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors from EnsurePDB propagate immediately with context
SetControllerReference errors on both create and update paths are returned

defensive_checklist.item: replicas=0 handled explicitly with documented rationale
Owner reference drift corrected on update path
Labels and annotations merged (not replaced) to preserve user-added metadata

suggested_improvements: D2: EnsurePDB uses reflect.DeepEqual for spec comparison while other Ensure* functions (database, secrets, tls) use apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual. For PDB specs this is functionally equivalent (no resource.Quantity or semantic types), but switching to apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual would be more consistent across packages. Not actionable now since reflect is already imported for EnsureDeployment selector comparison in the same file.
